hypothesis: DSTATE_CONFOUND_PREREG.md (H-3) Option 1 -- THE ATTRIBUTION CELL. The param ladder bundles a second architectural axis: d_state = 64/64/128 at 14M/98M/392M, so the DeltaNet state quadruples at the one interval the 3-point fit must span. The fit is SATURATED (3 points, 3 params, zero residual df) and in closed form beta_hat = (M_98 - M_14)/(x_98 - x_14) EXACTLY -- the 392M rung contributes ZERO information to the parameter slope, and the d_state step is perfectly aliased with curvature. A RISES verdict therefore CANNOT be attributed to parameter count, and no re-analysis fixes it. Rung Y (392M-scale params at d_state=64) completes a clean 3-rung d_state=64 ladder {14M, 98M, Y} -- second axis held fixed, GATE-A flips FAIL -> PASS -- at a 0.46% power cost, AND turns the existing 392M (d_state=128) rung into a matched-params (1.609% mismatch) measurement of the state-width effect, which is a first-class result on this program's own fast-weight-capacity thesis. Does not pre-judge the verdict; it makes an ATTRIBUTABLE one possible.

notes: PARAMS: 385,564,672 -- MEASURED by instantiating the real DeltaNetLM (dm=1536, ds=64, L=16, vocab 50257) on the box, NOT by arithmetic. Method validated: the same instantiation reproduces the existing 392M rung at 391,869,440 EXACTLY (S21 V-1). NOTE the pre-reg's own hand arithmetic says 385,577,984 and is WRONG by 13,312 (= 16 layers x 832): it omits the d_state-scaled short conv (3*d_state*conv_size = 768/layer) and a d_state-wide norm (64/layer). Immaterial (delta vs 392M = -1.609%, S_xx = 1.0446 clean vs 1.0542 confounded, power loss 0.46% -- all reproduce the pre-reg's S4) but recorded so it is not 'corrected' back. COST: MEASURED 0.840 s/step at d_state=128 from the two LIVE 392M jobs on this box (029: 39,454s/47,000 steps; 030: 38,302s/45,600 steps, both including startup and real 8-way contention). d_state=64 is priced at the SAME rate -- NOT a speedup assumption: every d_state-dependent cost term is monotone non-decreasing in d_state while the dominant FFN (8*d_model^2) and 50,257-way head are unchanged, so 0.840 s/step is a rigorous UPPER BOUND. x 67,547 steps = 15.76 GPU-h/cell, 31.5 for the pair. --internal-timeout 86400s (24.0h) = 1.52x that conservative bound and ~1.8x the likely true need -- MORE headroom than 029/030's 72000s (1.27x), on purpose: the mispriced-timeout bug has already cost this project two runs (job 200's 160000s vs a true ~71h need, ~44 GPU-h burned; the _fixscale_cell 36000s default vs 392M's 15.69h need). A too-small timeout guarantees a wasted run; a too-large one only burns extra on an already-pathological job. ARM/CONFIG/SEED/BUDGET match cells 029/030 exactly (per_token, lambda 0.58, seed 3, 67,547 steps, batch 32, seq 512, verified by reading their deployed specs on the box); the ONLY difference is d_state 128 -> 64, so the common-slice token match (T = 1.10669B) holds BY CONSTRUCTION and T does not move. Ids 033 sort above the whole pending backlog (400-431, 990) so they claim the first freed GPUs; they cannot preempt anything, since queue_worker.sh only claims when its OWN gpu is free and never evicts.
